content:
# Recommendations — for the iter-022 plan agent

## CRITICAL / HIGH (must-fix-this-iter from subagents)

1. **[lean-auditor must-fix] Stale L4 comment — `FlatteningStratification.lean:530–532.`**
   Inside the now-CLOSED L4 theorem `exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`, the iter-018
   banner still reads *"The remaining `sorry` is the denominator-clearing assembly."* L4 was closed
   axiom-clean this iter — there is no sorry. This will actively mislead any future reader checking
   L4's status (it is the SAME failure class as the iter-020 stale-comment major). The review agent
   cannot edit `.lean` files. **Action: direct the GF prover (or a refactor) to delete/update this
   banner when GF is next touched.** No correctness impact; comment-only.

2. **[lean-vs-blueprint-checker GF — major] Blueprint step-2 of `lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators`
   describes the WRONG Mathlib route.** The chapter (and its `% NOTE`) says the `g1` witness is a
   Finset-fold of `gf_clear_one_denominator` over coefficient polynomials. The landed Lean uses
   `IsIntegral.exists_multiple_integral_of_isLocalization` applied **per generator** (then
   `g1 := ∏_{x∈σ} a_x`, `g := g0·g1`). A future prover reading step 2 would reach for the wrong API.
   **Action: dispatch a blueprint-writer for `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` to rewrite step 2**
   to the per-generator `exists_multiple_integral_of_isLocalization` route (concrete replacement text
   is in the checker report). The lemma is closed, so this is a correspondence/accuracy fix, not a
   gate blocker — but it should land before any downstream prover relies on the chapter.

## HIGH — closest-to-completion proving target

3. **GF `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` B/𝔭 cascade @2021 is the highest-leverage GF prove.** L1/L4/L5 are
   ALL closed + axiom-clean, so this node is pure assembly EXCEPT one genuine new residual: the
   **ring-localisation ↔ module-localisation bridge** (`Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)` vs
   `LocalizedModule (powers g) C`) in step 4 of feeding `C := B⧸p` to L5. **Cheapest path:** a
   blueprint-writer round pinning the `IsLocalizedModule`-uniqueness iso
   `LocalizedModule (powers g) C ≃ Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`, THEN a prove pass (steps
   1–4 in the code comment @1997). Closing it makes GF-alg DONE → opens GF-geo `genericFlatness`
   @2109 (which owes a finite-affine-cover chapter section first).

## HIGH — FBC frontier

4. **FBC `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` @1551 — prove pass, blueprint is ADEQUATE** (checker
   confirmed the chapter names `conjugateEquiv_counit_symm` + the exact adjunction data + both
   sub-obligations). The first attempt isolated the goal correctly (2-`rw` reframing). Next: the
   counit-conjugate close — reproduce the Seam-1 skeleton for the counit, inline piece (a) via the
   PROVED `…_legs_unitExpand`/`_gammaDistribute` + `gammaMap_pushforwardComp_*` + Seam-1's
   `base_change_mate_unit_value`, close piece (b) by generator `ext` against
   `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`. **If it resists one prove pass, effort-break into (a) and (b)**
   (they are independent). Do NOT re-dispatch a whole-goal or per-generator-brute-force attempt
   (both confirmed dead ends).

## MEDIUM — debt the planner owns

5. **sync_leanok GF chapter resolution failure (systemic).** `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`
   has **0** `\leanok` markers across ALL blocks, including L4 (closed this iter), L5 (iter-017), and
   `isLocalization_lift_injective` (iter-019) — all sorry-free, axiom-clean, **public** theorems. The
   iter-021 sync only touched `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`. This is broader than the known "11 private
   Nagata helpers" debt (those names are mangled by `private`, item 6) — even public decls are
   unmarked. The dashboard materially under-reports GF. **Action: investigate why sync_leanok skips
   the entire GF chapter** (decl→file map / chapter-scan / namespace resolution). `\leanok` is the
   sync's domain, not the review agent's — flagging for the sync owner / planner.

6. **[recurring 018–021] De-privatize the 11 GF Nagata helpers + 3 FBC `gammaMap_*` helpers.** They
   carry public `\lean{...}` pins but are `private` (names mangled → unverifiable by `\lean{}`
   checkers and invisible to sync). Confirmed by both GF and FBC checkers this iter. **Action: a
   `refactor` de-private pass in a GF/FBC-no-prover slot** (the FBC `gammaMap_*` ones are dead-code-
   adjacent, fold into the FBC dead-code-removal slot).

7. **FBC dead-code removal (queued from iter-021).** The orphaned `fstar_reindex` /
   `fstar_reindex_legs` apparatus (+ 5 superseded blueprint blocks) is referenced only in comments.
   Deferred this iter to avoid same-file conflict with the gstar prove. **Action: FBC-no-prover slot,
   Lean + blueprint together** (also fixes minor M3 below and the private `gammaMap_*` pins).

## MINOR (one-liners — `summary.md` notes)

- **[FBC checker]** Stale `\uses{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}` on
  `lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` — the landed proof inlines `cancelBaseChange`/`comm`, doesn't
  call that helper. Blueprint-writer can fix the `\uses{}` next GF/FBC writer round.
- **[auditor M1/M2]** FBC docstrings on `base_change_mate_section_identity` (@1573) and
  `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` (@1641) say the sorry is "below"; it is actually
  inside a called lemma several levels deep. Comment-precision only.
- **[auditor M3]** FBC @1537 "carries a dead `sorry`" on `fstar_reindex_legs` is ambiguous (means
  "blocked pending refactor", not "false claim"); a clarifying parenthetical would help. Resolved by
  the dead-code-removal slot (item 7).
- **[auditor M4]** `RelativeSpec.lean:~230` — `UniversalProperty` name implies a Yoneda
  `RepresentableBy` witness but the theorem proves only `IsAffineHom`; acknowledged in the docstring,
  no current downstream need. Watch if a consumer ever needs the full universal property.

## Coverage debt
- `archon dag-query unmatched` = **0**, `gaps` = **0**. No new uncovered Lean decls; nothing to
  blueprint for 1-to-1 correspondence this iter. (The 2 new GradedModule helpers from the file-split
  were blueprinted in the plan phase.)

## Blocked targets — do NOT re-assign without a structural change
- **FBC `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex` / `_legs` (whole-goal OR fine-grained)** — DEAD CODE,
  obviated by the iter-020 route swap. Citing it forfeits axiom-cleanliness. Removal only.
- **FBC per-generator brute force on `gstar_transpose`** (`ext x` then `rfl`/`simp`) — confirmed dead
  end (the geometric counit/pullback/Γ have no element-level normal form).
- **QUOT route-(a) κ-linear `liftQ` detectors** for degreewise-independence goals — scalar-ring clash
  (`S = MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` vs `κ`). Use route (b) (ambient homogeneous-component membership).
- **GF L4 `g0`-only finiteness witness** — generically false-typed; the `g := g0·g1` two-factor
  witness is mandatory (now landed).

## Reusable proof patterns discovered this iter (also added to PROJECT_STATUS Knowledge Base)
- One-call denominator clearing via `IsIntegral.exists_multiple_integral_of_isLocalization` per
  generator (NOT a per-coefficient `gf_clear_one_denominator` fold) is the L4 finiteness unlock.
- `algebraMap (MvPoly A) (MvPoly S) = MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A S)` by `rfl`; the `C`-pushforward
  needs `map_C` (NOT `rfl`).
- Inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`, bare `IsIntegral` clashes with a scheme-typed name — use
  `_root_.IsIntegral`.
